reasoning: At 8x zoom the central fire/spark starburst shows clear left-right asymmetry: the left flame arm has 2 orange pixels stacked while the right arm has only 1, and the lower arm extends 1px further than the upper. The boundary pixels where the fire meets the scroll parchment body show orange and red color bleed into what should be clean beige/tan cells. On the right edge of the scroll body there are 3-4 isolated light-gray pixels that appear to be unintentional AA remnants or noise rather than intentional shading — inconsistent with the otherwise clean 1px hard outline. The cross-hatch scroll texture lines have a subtle regularity artifact: the diagonal strokes maintain nearly machine-perfect 1px spacing without the micro-variation a human would introduce. No gradient shading violations (hard 3-tone palette on body), no melting details, no semantic failure (scroll + fire reads correctly). Sub-pixel features and pattern artifacts are present but minor. Overall: AI-assisted or AI-generated with imperfect cleanup; passes on semantics and palette discipline but fails on strict pixel-art symmetry and clean boundary requirements.
